reasoning: While President Trump announced on June 5, 2025, that he had accepted an invitation to visit China, no specific date has been set as of the June 15 cutoff. Historical precedent for state visits to China involves substantial lead time for logistics and security, typically announced weeks in advance. With only 15 days remaining in the window and the President's schedule showing him in Washington as late as June 12, a visit by July 1 is highly improbable. Furthermore, delegation-level trade talks in London (June 9-13) suggest that high-level engagement is currently handled by cabinet secretaries rather than a presidential visit to Beijing.
